disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Superficial partial-thickness graze — no deep gaping wound, no exposed fat/muscle/tendon, full joint movement, normal neurovascular status (not a deep laceration/fracture)
trigger_question_examples: Is it a surface graze, or a deep gaping cut with visible fat/muscle/tendon?
Can you move your knee fully, and is the foot warm with normal feeling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a surface graze, no deep gaping or visible fat/muscle; I can move my knee fully and the foot's fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Wound visibly contaminated with embedded gravel/dirt, and tetanus immunisation status uncertain/>10 years — needs thorough irrigation/debridement and a tetanus booster (in-person)
trigger_question_examples: Is there dirt/gravel embedded that you can't rinse out, and when was your last tetanus shot?
Can you get the wound properly clean at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's gravel embedded I can't get out, and I'm not sure of my last tetanus — probably over 10 years ago.
